section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pain to palpation of the right knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Pain to palpation is a physical exam finding elicited by the clinician, not a purely patient-reported symptom.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor observes edema in the right knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Correctly placed as an observed finding; no miscategorization.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor observes erythema in the right knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Correctly placed as an observed finding; no miscategorization.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's pain is in the left hand.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Location of pain is a symptom description and should be documented as patient-reported subjective information.
Current medications the patient is already taking belong in the Subjective section unless framed as a treatment change or order.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports taking tylenol for the pain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is patient-reported current medication use and should be in the Subjective section.
Correctly placed as part of the clinician's assessment/problem list.
Correctly placed as part of the clinician's assessment/problem list.
This is a patient-reported symptom and belongs in the Subjective section, not in the clinician assessment.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor orders an autoimmune panel.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Correctly placed as an order/plan item.
Correctly placed as counseling/home care instruction in the plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor prescribes ultram 50 milligrams every six hours as needed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Correctly placed as a new prescription in the plan.
Here it is again describing an existing medication, not a new order or change, so it belongs in the Subjective section.
